steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a problem: theater needs dialogue, but the original story conveyed internal thoughts without it. The underlined sentence begins "To transform..." and directly addresses how Yamauchi solved that specific problem. The right answer must describe that solution.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It describes how Yamauchi chose the actors who performed in the play.’: Casting is absent from the underlined sentence entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It offers information about how Yamauchi adapted her short story into a play.’: The sentence explains precisely how Yamauchi turned the story's internal thoughts into theatrical dialogue — the solution to the stated problem. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘It argues that Yamauchi's play influenced later playwrights.’: No literary influence is discussed in the underlined sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It explains why Yamauchi's short story is better known than the play adaptation is.’: Comparisons of fame do not appear in the underlined sentence. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
